title: Iowa vs Nebraska — Sweet 16 Viewing Guide
eventTag: NCAA Tournament • Sweet 16 • South Region • Houston, TX
extraInfo: Winner advances to the Elite Eight to face the winner of Texas vs Purdue

stakes.callout: Big Ten rivals. Rubber match. Both programs making history. Iowa just pulled off the biggest upset of the tournament, stunning #1 seed Florida 73-72 on a Folgueiras three with 4.5 seconds left — their first Sweet 16 in 27 years. Nebraska is in the Sweet 16 for the first time ever after going 0-8 in tournament games across 39 years before this week. They split the regular season series. Nebraska's star scorer Pryce Sandfort transferred from Iowa. This is a game neither fanbase thought was possible two weeks ago — and now it's the biggest basketball game in either program's recent history.

cards.label: The Rubber Match
cards.value: Iowa and Nebraska split the regular season: Iowa won 57-52 in Iowa City (Feb. 17) behind Stirtz's 25 points, then Nebraska won 84-75 in overtime in Lincoln (March 8) on Senior Day. Now they meet for the third time — with a trip to the Elite Eight on the line. Each team knows exactly what the other does.

cards.label: Iowa's Cinderella Run
cards.value: A #9 seed that beat #8 Clemson 67-61, then stunned #1 Florida 73-72 on a go-ahead corner three with 4.5 seconds left. Iowa's first Sweet 16 since 1999 — and only the second time they've beaten a #1 seed (first: Syracuse in 1980). Ben McCollum is 429-95 career (.818). The man wins everywhere.

cards.label: Nebraska's Historic Run
cards.value: Nebraska went 0-8 in tournament games across 39 years before this week. Now they've won two — demolishing Troy 76-47 (Sandfort: 7 threes, 23 points) and edging Vanderbilt 74-72 on Frager's layup with 2.2 seconds left. Fred Hoiberg cried at the podium after the Troy game thinking of his father. This is the first Sweet 16 in program history.

cards.label: The Sandfort Factor
cards.value: Pryce Sandfort was Iowa Mr. Basketball in 2023 and played two seasons at Iowa before transferring to Nebraska. He leads the Huskers with 17.8 PPG and .401 three-point shooting. This is his third game against his former team this season. In the first two: 13 points at Iowa (loss), 15 points at home (OT win). His brother Payton plays in the NBA for the Thunder.

styles.title: McCollum's Giant-Killers
bullets: They beat the #1 seed — on a last-second three: Iowa 73, Florida 72. Folgueiras hit a go-ahead corner three with 4.5 seconds left after Stirtz drew the defense and found him wide open. Iowa led for 66% of that game and shot 51% from the field (26-51). This team doesn't play scared.

Second-slowest pace in America: Iowa plays at 64.8 possessions per game (363rd nationally). McCollum wants every possession to matter. Against Florida, they slowed the tempo and frustrated the Gators' transition game. Against Nebraska, they'll try the same — limit possessions and make every one count.

Banks is the X-factor: Tavion Banks had 20 points on 7-of-10 shooting against Florida. He shoots 47.2% from three on the season and is Iowa's best scoring threat alongside Stirtz. When Banks hits early, Iowa's spacing opens everything up.

Stirtz impacts games even when cold: Against Florida, Stirtz went 0-of-9 from three but still had 13 points, 5 rebounds, and 5 assists — plus the game-winning assist to Folgueiras. Against Clemson he was 4-of-17 but led the team in scoring and assists. Iowa's system doesn't collapse when the star struggles.

Weakness — rebounding inconsistency: Iowa outrebounded Nebraska 37-24 in Iowa City but was more even on the glass in Lincoln. Against Florida they tied 27-27. If Nebraska controls the boards with Mast (6-10, 250) and Buyuktuncel (6-9, 245), Iowa loses second-chance opportunities they need.

styles.title: Elite Defense, Historic Heart
bullets: Suffocating defense: Nebraska allows just 66.2 PPG (22nd nationally in defensive efficiency). They held Troy to 28.3% shooting and 47 points. Against Vanderbilt's 86 PPG offense, they held them to 72 on 41.4% shooting. Iowa scored just 52 points in Iowa City and 75 in overtime in Lincoln. Nebraska's defense travels.

Sandfort is a walking bucket: Pryce Sandfort averages 17.8 PPG on .401 three-point shooting and ranks 6th nationally in threes per game (3.6). He hit 7 threes for 23 points against Troy and had 15 against Vanderbilt. Against his former Iowa teammates, he knows every tendency.

Frager has the clutch gene: Braden Frager — the Big Ten Sixth Man of the Year — hit the game-winning layup with 2.2 seconds left against Vanderbilt to send Nebraska to the Sweet 16. He had 15 points in that game and 13 against Troy. The Lincoln native grew up watching Husker basketball from the rafters.

Ball security: Sam Hoiberg's 6.0:1 assist-to-turnover ratio (2nd nationally) keeps Nebraska's offense organized. Against Troy, Nebraska committed just 6 turnovers. Against Vanderbilt, Hoiberg had 8 assists. The coach's son is the steadiest hand in the tournament.

Weakness — the late-season slide: Nebraska started 20-0 then went 6-6 in their final 12 regular season games. Turnovers, rebounding, and free throw drawing all regressed. They've looked sharp in the tournament, but Iowa beat them during that slide (57-52). Which Nebraska shows up?

keys.title: Iowa's pace control vs. Nebraska's defensive pressure.
keys.description: Iowa plays at the second-slowest pace in America (64.8 possessions per game). Nebraska's defense thrives on forcing difficult shots and limiting scoring. In their first meeting, Iowa won 57-52 in a crawl — exactly the game they want. In the second meeting, Nebraska pushed the pace to 84-75 OT. The team that dictates tempo wins this game. If it stays in the low 60s, Iowa's efficiency carries the day. If Nebraska pushes it into the 70s, their depth and defense take over.

keys.title: Stirtz doesn't need to score to win.
keys.description: Bennett Stirtz has gone 4-of-17 against Clemson and 0-of-9 from three against Florida — and Iowa won both games. He had 16 points, 5 assists against Clemson and 13 points, 5 assists, 5 rebounds against Florida. He makes the right pass (the Folgueiras assist), controls tempo, and gets to the line. Nebraska's Sam Hoiberg will guard him — and Hoiberg is All-Big Ten Defensive Team. If Stirtz scores 20+, Iowa wins easily. But even if Hoiberg contains him, Stirtz can still set up Banks, Koch, and Folgueiras.

keys.title: The Sandfort revenge game — or redemption game.
keys.description: Pryce Sandfort transferred from Iowa to Nebraska and leads the Huskers with 17.8 PPG. He scored 13 in the loss at Iowa and 15 in the OT win at home. He hit 7 threes against Troy and 3 against Vanderbilt in the tournament. Iowa knows his game inside and out — he was their teammate. If Iowa can limit him to single-digit threes, Nebraska's offense loses its engine. If Sandfort gets hot against his old team, it could be a legacy-defining performance.

keys.title: Rebounding decides close games.
keys.description: Iowa outrebounded Nebraska 37-24 in their first meeting and won by 5. In the second meeting, the rebounding was more even and Nebraska won in overtime. In the tournament, Iowa tied Florida 27-27 while Nebraska outrebounded both Troy (37-33) and Vanderbilt. Rienk Mast (6-10, 250) and Buyuktuncel (6-9, 245) give Nebraska a physical interior edge. If Iowa gets dominated on the glass, they won't get the second-chance points they need in a low-possession game.

keys.title: Two teams riding emotional highs — which one crashes?
keys.description: Iowa just pulled off a miracle upset of #1 Florida. Nebraska just won its first-ever tournament games after 39 years of futility. Both programs are in uncharted territory. The emotional peak from Sunday's wins can either fuel Thursday's performance or create a letdown. Iowa has the momentum of the ultimate Cinderella — they've already exceeded expectations. Nebraska has the pressure of being the higher seed and the favored team. In a rubber match where both teams know each other, composure in the final five minutes will separate the winner.

prediction.summary: This is the best kind of March Madness game: two Big Ten rivals who split the regular season, meeting in the Sweet 16 with everything on the line. Iowa plays at the second-slowest pace in America and just proved they can beat anyone by knocking off #1 Florida. Nebraska has elite defense (22nd nationally) and a star in Sandfort who knows Iowa's system from the inside. The key tension: Iowa wants this in the 50s with a half-court grind. Nebraska wants it in the 60s where their defense and depth advantage takes over. Stirtz doesn't need to score 25 — he proved that against Florida. But Nebraska's defense is far better than Florida's, and Hoiberg will make Stirtz work for every touch. Expect a physical, tight, low-scoring game where the final five minutes feel like a season.

phases.title: Opening 10 Minutes
phases.description: Nebraska's defense sets the tone early. Sam Hoiberg picks up Stirtz full-court and makes him work just to get the ball. Sandfort hits an early three against his old team — the Toyota Center crowd buzzes. But Iowa isn't rattled. They've beaten Clemson and Florida already this tournament. Banks answers with a mid-range jumper, and Koch drains a corner three. The game is tight — Nebraska 14, Iowa 12.

phases.title: Mid-First Half to Halftime
phases.description: Iowa slows the pace to a crawl — exactly what they want. McCollum's system grinds possessions, runs clock, and forces Nebraska to guard for 25+ seconds each trip. Stirtz finds Folgueiras cutting for a layup. Manyawu converts at the rim. But Sandfort hits another three and Mast muscles in a hook shot. Nebraska's defense holds Iowa to 38% shooting in the half. Halftime: Nebraska 28, Iowa 25. A Sweet 16 game playing out exactly like a Big Ten rivalry game should.

phases.title: Second Half — The Runs
phases.description: Nebraska opens the second half with a 7-0 run — Frager hits a three off the bench, Hoiberg threads a pass to Buyuktuncel for a layup, and Sandfort converts a fast break. Lead: 35-25. Iowa calls timeout. McCollum adjusts. Banks hits back-to-back threes to cut it to 4. Stirtz drives and finds Koch for another three. Suddenly it's 39-37. The momentum swings become violent — Nebraska pushes it to 8, Iowa cuts it to 2, back and forth.

phases.title: Final 5 Minutes
phases.description: Tied at 55 with 4:30 left. Every possession is a season. Sandfort misses a contested three — Iowa rebounds. Stirtz drives left, draws two defenders, kicks to Banks who drains a three from the wing. Iowa 58, Nebraska 55. Mast answers with a hook. 58-57. Hoiberg steals a lazy Iowa pass — Frager converts in transition. 59-58 Nebraska. Under a minute. Stirtz runs clock, drives, gets fouled. Hits both free throws. 60-59 Iowa. Nebraska inbounds to Sandfort — his pull-up three rims out. Iowa rebounds. Game.

projectedScore.rationale: Iowa's pace control and tournament composure — they've won two elimination games already, including over a #1 seed — give them a slight edge in a rock fight. Nebraska's defense is elite, but Iowa has proven they can win ugly. Stirtz doesn't need to be the scorer; he's the facilitator. Banks and Koch provide the shooting, and Folgueiras has already hit the biggest shot of the tournament. Nebraska's first-ever Sweet 16 is a monumental achievement regardless, but the pressure of being the favorite in a rubber match against a Cinderella may be the difference.

players.name: Bennett Stirtz
players.stats: 19.9 PPG • 4.4 APG • 5 AST vs Florida • .483 FG%
players.meta: G • 6-4 • Senior • All-Big Ten, AP All-America HM
players.bio:
From Liberty, Missouri. Has followed coach McCollum across three programs: Northwest Missouri State (DII), Drake, and Iowa. Was Missouri Valley Player of the Year at Drake. Named All-Big Ten and AP All-America honorable mention. In this tournament, he's been the ultimate floor general: 16 points and 5 assists against Clemson (4-of-17 FG), then 13 points, 5 assists, and the game-winning pass to Folgueiras against Florida (0-of-9 from three). He doesn't need to score to win. Against Nebraska in the regular season, he had 25 points in the Iowa City win. Shoots 84.5% from the line.
Read more →

players.name: Tavion Banks
players.bio: Senior wing who shoots 47.2% from three on the season. Had a breakout 20-point game against Florida (7-of-10 FG) that fueled the upset. At 6-7, he stretches the floor and provides rebounding (6 boards vs. Florida). When Banks is hitting, Iowa's spacing becomes nearly impossible to defend. Nebraska must account for him every possession.

players.name: Alvaro Folgueiras
players.bio: The man who hit the shot. With 4.5 seconds left and Iowa trailing 72-70, Folgueiras caught a pass from Stirtz in the right corner and drained the go-ahead three to beat #1 Florida. Afterward, he dedicated the moment to his family: "This one is for her and my dad. He's watching up there." From Spain, he averages 2.3 APG from the forward position. Scored 14 against both Clemson and Florida. Went 7-of-7 from the free throw line against Clemson. The tournament's most clutch player.

players.name: Cooper Koch
players.bio: Redshirt freshman who scored 12 points on 4-of-6 three-point shooting against Florida. Had a career-high 19 with five threes in the Big Ten Tournament win over Maryland. His first career double-double (14 pts, 10 reb) came against Ole Miss. Koch's emergence as a reliable shooter gives Iowa four perimeter threats — and that spacing is what makes McCollum's system hum.

players.name: Pryce Sandfort
players.meta: F • 6-7 • Junior • Iowa Transfer • First-Team All-Big Ten
players.bio:
From Waukee, Iowa — 2023 Iowa Mr. Basketball. Played two seasons at Iowa before transferring to Nebraska. Brother Payton plays for the OKC Thunder. Now he's Nebraska's leading scorer at 17.8 PPG and ranks 6th nationally in threes per game (3.6). Tournament stats: 23 points with 7 threes against Troy, 15 points against Vanderbilt. Against Iowa in the regular season: 13 points in the loss, 15 in the OT win. Lute Olson Award finalist. This is the game he's circled all year — facing the program he left behind, in the Sweet 16.
Read more →

players.name: Sam Hoiberg
players.stats: 9.6 PPG • 4.3 APG • 6.0:1 A/TO • All-Big Ten Defense
players.meta: G • 6-0 • Senior • Coach's Son • Walk-On to Starter
players.bio:
Son of head coach Fred Hoiberg. Joined Nebraska as a walk-on with zero Division I scholarship offers. Twin brother Charlie is a graduate manager. Now he starts all games and has the 2nd-best assist-to-turnover ratio in America (6.0:1). Made the All-Big Ten Defensive Team. Against Troy: 9 rebounds and 4 assists. Against Vanderbilt: 8 assists and a crucial putback that tied the game late. His defensive assignment Thursday: slowing down Bennett Stirtz. If he can, Nebraska wins.
Read more →

players.name: Braden Frager
players.stats: 11.6 PPG • Big Ten 6th Man of the Year • Game-Winner vs Vanderbilt
players.meta: F • Redshirt Freshman • Lincoln, NE • Hometown Hero
players.bio: Grew up in Lincoln, Nebraska, watching Husker basketball from the rafters. Now he's the Big Ten Sixth Man of the Year — the first Nebraska player to win the award. Hit the game-winning driving layup with 2.2 seconds left against Vanderbilt to send Nebraska to the Sweet 16. Tournament stats: 13 points vs. Troy, 15 vs. Vanderbilt. Career-high 23 points (7 threes) vs. Oregon. A lifelong Husker fan living his childhood dream on the biggest stage.

players.name: Rienk Mast
players.bio:
From Groningen, Netherlands. Switched from korfball to basketball. After 4 years at Bradley, transferred to Nebraska — then missed the entire 2024-25 season after knee surgery using cadaver cartilage. Returned after 575 days to score 31 in his first game back. Recorded the 3rd triple-double in Nebraska history (18/12/10 vs. FIU). Tournament: 11/6/7 vs. Troy, 13/4/1 vs. Vanderbilt. His 6-10, 250-pound frame gives Nebraska a physical advantage Iowa's frontcourt can't match.
Read more →

funFacts.title: The Rubber Match
funFacts.body: Iowa and Nebraska split the regular season: Iowa won 57-52 in Iowa City and Nebraska won 84-75 in overtime in Lincoln. This is the third meeting — and the first time these Big Ten rivals have ever played each other in the NCAA Tournament.

funFacts.title: Iowa's Miracle Against Florida
funFacts.body: Down 72-70 with 4.5 seconds left, Stirtz broke the press and found Folgueiras in the corner for a go-ahead three. It was the first game-winner in the final 5 seconds against a #1 seed since Kris Jenkins' 2016 national championship buzzer-beater for Villanova.

funFacts.title: "I Think of My Dad" (Nebraska)
funFacts.body: After winning Nebraska's first-ever tournament game (the program was 0-8 across 39 years), Fred Hoiberg choked up at the podium: "I think of my dad, who's in a home right now. I hope he got a chance to watch this one."

funFacts.title: The Sandfort Transfer
funFacts.body: Pryce Sandfort was Iowa Mr. Basketball in 2023, played two years at Iowa, then transferred to Nebraska. His brother Payton plays for the OKC Thunder. Now Pryce leads Nebraska with 17.8 PPG and faces his former team in the Sweet 16.

funFacts.title: Stirtz Doesn't Need to Score
funFacts.body: Bennett Stirtz went 4-of-17 against Clemson and 0-of-9 from three against Florida — and Iowa won both games. He had 5 assists in each game. Against Florida, he made the pass that won the game. The ultimate point guard.

funFacts.title: Cadaver Cartilage Comeback (Nebraska)
funFacts.body: Rienk Mast missed an entire season after knee surgery using cadaver cartilage. After 575 days away, he scored 31 points with 7 threes in his first game back. Then recorded only the 3rd triple-double in Nebraska history. Now he's in the Sweet 16.

funFacts.title: Father Coaching Son (Nebraska)
funFacts.body: Fred Hoiberg coaches his son Sam — a walk-on with zero D-I offers who now starts every game and has the 2nd-best assist-to-turnover ratio in America (6.0:1). Twin brother Charlie is a graduate manager. The whole family is on this ride.

funFacts.title: Four DII National Titles (Iowa)
funFacts.body: Iowa coach Ben McCollum won four DII national championships at Northwest Missouri State. His career record of 429-95 (.818) is the fifth-best all-time among collegiate coaches. Bennett Stirtz followed him across three programs. Now they're in the Sweet 16.

funFacts.title: Frager's Childhood Dream (Nebraska)
funFacts.body: Braden Frager grew up in Lincoln watching Husker basketball from the rafters. He hit the game-winning layup with 2.2 seconds left against Vanderbilt to send Nebraska to its first Sweet 16. "Football, basketball, everything. I was at a lot of the games." Now he is the game.

funFacts.title: The Spanish Game-Winner (Iowa)
funFacts.body: Alvaro Folgueiras, from Spain, hit the biggest shot of the 2026 tournament — a corner three with 4.5 seconds left to beat #1 Florida. He dedicated it to his family: "This one is for her and my dad. He's watching up there."

funFacts.title: Football Schools, Basketball Dreams
funFacts.body: Nebraska has 5 football national championships and had never won a tournament basketball game before this week. Iowa's Kinnick Stadium (football) seats 69,250. Yet here both programs are — playing for the Elite Eight. March does funny things.

funFacts.title: Iowa Leads the All-Time Series 28-15
funFacts.body: These programs first played on January 28, 1907 — a 27-17 Nebraska win in Lincoln. The series resumed when Nebraska joined the Big Ten in 2011. Iowa had won 10 of 11 in Iowa City before Nebraska's recent surge. Now they meet in the tournament for the first time.

history.callout: Two Big Ten rivals meeting in the NCAA Tournament for the first time ever. Iowa is in the Sweet 16 for the first time since 1999. Nebraska is in the Sweet 16 for the first time in program history. Iowa leads the all-time series 28-15, dating back to 1907. They split the 2025-26 regular season. Nebraska's star scorer transferred from Iowa. This is the biggest basketball game in either program's recent memory.

timeline.text: Iowa and Nebraska first meet on the basketball court — January 28, 1907 in Lincoln. Nebraska wins 27-17. They would play sporadically for the next century.
The schools schedule yearly series in the 1940s, 1950s, and a home-and-home in the 1970s. Then a 35-year hiatus with no games at all.
Nebraska joins the Big Ten, renewing the rivalry as conference opponents. The schools play twice per year.
Iowa wins 57-52 in Iowa City (Feb. 17). Nebraska wins 84-75 OT in Lincoln (March 8). Now they meet for a third time — in the Sweet 16. First-ever tournament meeting between the programs.

subsections.title: Two Programs Making History
subsections.content:
Iowa: The Hawkeyes have made 30 NCAA Tournament appearances with three Final Four trips (1956, 1980, 1987). But they hadn't reached the Sweet 16 since 1999 — a 27-year drought. Ben McCollum, in his first year, has taken Iowa further than any coach since Tom Davis. The stunning upset of #1 Florida — on Folgueiras' corner three with 4.5 seconds left — instantly became one of the greatest moments in Iowa basketball history.

Nebraska: The Cornhuskers went 0-8 in tournament games across 39 years — the only power conference team to never win one. Now they've won two in the same week, reaching the Sweet 16 for the first time ever. Fred Hoiberg went from 7-25 in Year 1 to 28-6 in Year 7. After the Troy game, he cried thinking of his father. After the Vanderbilt game, Frager's buzzer-beater layup sent Nebraska to Houston. This is a program transformed.

subsections.title: The Transfer That Connects Them
subsections.content:
Pryce Sandfort was the 2023 Iowa Mr. Basketball at Waukee Northwest, just outside Des Moines. He played two seasons at Iowa, shooting 40% from three as a sophomore. Then he transferred to Nebraska — following Fred Hoiberg, who had recruited him originally. At Nebraska, Sandfort became a star: 17.8 PPG, First-Team All-Big Ten, Lute Olson Award finalist.

In two games against Iowa this season, Sandfort averaged 14 points. Now he faces his former team with a trip to the Elite Eight on the line. His brother Payton played at Iowa before being drafted by the OKC Thunder. The Sandfort family bleeds both black-and-gold and scarlet — Thursday forces them to pick a side.
